# Welcome {.unnumbered}

This is a portfolio of **statistical modeling and machine-learning projects** built in R and Python during my time at Penn State. Each chapter takes a real dataset, builds and evaluates models, and rebuilds every figure as an **interactive [plotly](https://plotly.com/) visualization** — paired with a plain-language explanation of what the analysis is doing and why.

## What's inside

The work is grouped into two parts: foundational statistical modeling in R, and machine-learning models built in Python.

### Part I — Data Analysis & Regression (R)

Built with R, `tidyverse`, `glmnet`, and `FNN`.

| Chapter | Focus | Key methods |
|---|---|---|
| [Data Wrangling](chapters/01-data-wrangling.qmd) | Querying baseball career records | `dplyr` aggregation, grouping & filtering |
| [Linear Regression](chapters/02-linear-regression.qmd) | Insurance, used cars & housing prices | Nested model comparison, validation metrics (MSE/RMSE/MAE), multiple regression |
| [Regularization & kNN](chapters/03-regularization-knn.qmd) | Salary & housing prediction | LASSO, backward elimination, k-nearest-neighbors regression |

### Part II — Machine Learning (Python)

Built with Python, `scikit-learn`, and `plotly`.

| Chapter | Focus | Key methods |
|---|---|---|
| [Gaussian Naïve Bayes](chapters/04-naive-bayes.qmd) | Iris classification from scratch | Gaussian likelihoods, Bayes' theorem, confusion matrix |
| [Neural Networks](chapters/05-neural-network.qmd) | Dry Bean variety classification | `MLPClassifier`, feature scaling, hyperparameter tuning |
| [Random Forests](chapters/06-random-forest.qmd) | Gini impurity & feature importance | Decision trees, MDI vs permutation importance |
| [ROC & Cross-Validation](chapters/07-roc-kfold.qmd) | Corporate bankruptcy prediction | Stratified k-fold, ROC/AUC, normalization |
| [Decision Trees](chapters/08-decision-trees.qmd) | Breast-cancer diagnosis | Entropy/information gain, hyperparameter effects |

## How to read this portfolio

- Use the **sidebar** to jump between chapters, or the **search** box (top of the sidebar) to find a topic.
- Most charts are **interactive** — hover for tooltips, click legend entries to toggle series, and drag to zoom.
- Code is **folded by default**. Click **▸ Code** above any output to reveal the source, or use the **code menu** (top-right) to show all code at once.

## Reproducibility

Every chapter is self-contained and fully reproducible — each one loads its own data and computes its results at render time. Datasets live in the `Files/` directory of the [source repository](https://github.com/arihaanbarua/machine-learning-portfolio); the machine-learning chapters pull benchmark datasets directly from the [UCI Machine Learning Repository](https://archive.ics.uci.edu/).
